sampledesc: similar to most of the granite of the Round Mountain lobe, but it is more biotite rich (15%). It contains about 2.5 percent black opaque minerals (mostly hematite), 0.25 percent apatite, 0.1 percent monazite, and traces of zircon, fluorite, pyrite, and probably other sulfides. The accessory minerals, including fluorite and sulfides, are clustered together with aggregates of ragged biotite and hematite crystals
citation: Shawe, D.R., Marvin, R.F., Andriessen, P.A.M., Mehnert, H.H., and Merritt, V.M., 1986, Ages of igneous and hydrothermal events in the Round Mountain and Manhattan gold districts, Nye County, Nevada: Economic Geology, v. 81, no. 2, p. 388–407.
localitydesc: Darrough prospect in Shoshone Canyon, 2.5 km from the mouth of the canyon

sampledesc: Hydrothermally altered and mineralized Oligocene rhyolite dike in the Round Mountain lobe of the Cretaceous granite of Shoshone Mountain. The rhyolite consists of small phenocrysts of quartz and altered feldspars in an aphanitic groundmass. Zircon is a minor accessory mineral. Magnetite, hematite, epidote, tourmaline, and adularia fill fractures and rhyolite breccia.
citation: Shawe, D.R., Marvin, R.F., Andriessen, P.A.M., Mehnert, H.H., and Merritt, V.M., 1986, Ages of igneous and hydrothermal events in the Round Mountain and Manhattan gold districts, Nye County, Nevada: Economic Geology, v. 81, no. 2, p. 388–407.
fieldname: Hydrothermally altered and mineralized rhyolite dike
localitydesc: Prospect 0.4 km east of Dry Canyon and 0.5 km southeast of the middle Tertiary granodiorite stock

sampledesc: Oligocene and Miocene porphyritic rhyolite plug intruded in the ring-fracture zone of the Oligocene and Miocene Mount Jefferson caldera. Hydrothermally altered rhyolite contains thin veinlets of adularia and adularia crystals that formed on fracture surfaces. Rhyolite contains minor accessory zircon.
citation: Shawe, D.R., Marvin, R.F., Andriessen, P.A.M., Mehnert, H.H., and Merritt, V.M., 1986, Ages of igneous and hydrothermal events in the Round Mountain and Manhattan gold districts, Nye County, Nevada: Economic Geology, v. 81, no. 2, p. 388–407.
localitydesc: Jefferson Canyon, old Jefferson mining district

sampledesc: Oligocene and Miocene biotite-quartz latite ash-flow tuff of the unnamed youngest stratigraphic unit of volcanic rocks. Petrographically similar to other rocks of the unit but with higher sodic plagioclase content (about 25%) and lower quartz and sanidine content than is normal; contains minor accessory zircon and apatite.
citation: Shawe, D.R., Marvin, R.F., Andriessen, P.A.M., Mehnert, H.H., and Merritt, V.M., 1986, Ages of igneous and hydrothermal events in the Round Mountain and Manhattan gold districts, Nye County, Nevada: Economic Geology, v. 81, no. 2, p. 388–407.

sampledesc: Miocene olive-brown andesite emplaced as an east-trending stock, 1 X 3 km in areal extent. Calcic plagioclase forms phenocrysts as well as small laths in the groundmass of the rock; augite and black opaque (iron-titanium oxide) minerals also form small crystals in the groundmass; apatite is a minor accessory mineral.

In addition to major minerals which are the same as those in the Shoshone Mountain unit, this rock contains accessory black opaque (iron-titanium oxide) minerals, apatite, monazite, and zircon.
citation: Shawe, D.R., Marvin, R.F., Andriessen, P.A.M., Mehnert, H.H., and Merritt, V.M., 1986, Ages of igneous and hydrothermal events in the Round Mountain and Manhattan gold districts, Nye County, Nevada: Economic Geology, v. 81, no. 2, p. 388–407.
localitydesc: 3.5 km south-southeast of Manhattan

sampledesc: Oligocene medium gray granodiorite that forms an irregular crescent-shaped stock, 2 km long and as much as 0.6 km wide, within the Cretaceous granite of Shoshone Mountain and transecting the Oligocene rhyolite dike swarm. Intermediate plagioclase, quartz, biotite, orthoclase, augite, and hornblende are principal minerals; iron-titanium oxides, apatite, sphene, and zircon are accessories.
citation: Shawe, D.R., Marvin, R.F., Andriessen, P.A.M., Mehnert, H.H., and Merritt, V.M., 1986, Ages of igneous and hydrothermal events in the Round Mountain and Manhattan gold districts, Nye County, Nevada: Economic Geology, v. 81, no. 2, p. 388–407.

sampledesc: Oligocene and Miocene brown densely welded rhyolitic ash-flow tuff of the unnamed youngest stratigraphic unit of volcanic rocks. Crystal-poor rock with sanidine, quartz, sodic plagioclase, and biotite along with devitrified glass and lithic fragments in a groundmass of flattened and strongly welded pumice fragments and glass shards. Accessories are black opaque (iron-titanium oxide) minerals and zircon.
citation: Shawe, D.R., Marvin, R.F., Andriessen, P.A.M., Mehnert, H.H., and Merritt, V.M., 1986, Ages of igneous and hydrothermal events in the Round Mountain and Manhattan gold districts, Nye County, Nevada: Economic Geology, v. 81, no. 2, p. 388–407.
fieldname: brown densely welded rhyolitic ash-flow tuff
localitydesc: 0.4 km southwest of peak 9199 and 1.2 km east of Bald Mountain

sampledesc: Oligocene and Miocene light greenishbuff quartz latitic or rhyolitic ash tuff of the unnamed youngest stratigraphic unit of volcanic rocks. Similar to other ash-tuff rocks of the unit but nearly devoid of biotite. Accessories are black opaque (iron-titanium oxide) minerals and zircon.
citation: Shawe, D.R., Marvin, R.F., Andriessen, P.A.M., Mehnert, H.H., and Merritt, V.M., 1986, Ages of igneous and hydrothermal events in the Round Mountain and Manhattan gold districts, Nye County, Nevada: Economic Geology, v. 81, no. 2, p. 388–407.
localitydesc: 0.6 km southwest of peak 9199 and 1.2 km east-southeast of Bald Mountain

sampledesc: Round Mountain lobe of the Cretaceous granite of Shoshone Mountain, within 2 m of a quartz vein containing pyrite, sphalerite, and aikinite and within 80 m of a rhyolite dike of the northeast trending dike swarm. Sample is typical of granite of the Round Mountain lobe; zircon and apatite are minor accessory minerals. Near by granite contains disseminated pyrite and molybdenite and thin veinlets of pyrite
citation: Shawe, D.R., Marvin, R.F., Andriessen, P.A.M., Mehnert, H.H., and Merritt, V.M., 1986, Ages of igneous and hydrothermal events in the Round Mountain and Manhattan gold districts, Nye County, Nevada: Economic Geology, v. 81, no. 2, p. 388–407.

sampledesc: Light brownish-gray rhyolitic welded ash-flow tuff, part of the tuff of Mount Jefferson. Tuff contains moderate amounts of quartz, sanidine, and biotite phenocrysts and some altered pumice fragments, set in an aphanitic groundmass. Black opaque (iron-titanium oxide) minerals, zircon, and apatite are minor accessories
